experiments.reasoning: The passage describes a deliberate observation of a luminous phenomenon in a specific piece of meat under controlled conditions (dark room), involving a systematic procedure (placing the meat in a dark corner, observing its luminous properties). The author explicitly mentions 'tryals' and 'Observations,' which aligns with the definition of an experiment.
experiments.description: The experiment involved observing the luminous properties of a veal neck, which had been hung in a dark room. The author noted that the meat emitted a luminous glow resembling that of rotten wood or stinking fish, likely due to bioluminescence or some other natural phenomenon involving the meat.
experiments.quote: ‘I plainly saw, both with wonder and delight, that the joint of meat did in divers places shine like rotten Wood or stinking Fish’
experiments.outcome: The meat exhibited a luminous phenomenon, though the exact cause or mechanism was not explored further in this passage.
experiments.location: A corner of the author's chamber (room) in his house.
